channel.title: Emergence on Keel
channel.description: Recent content in Emergence on Keel

item.title: The Ground Remembers
item.description: There’s a word for what ants do when they find food: stigmergy. Pierre-Paul Grassé coined it in 1959 watching termites build cathedrals without blueprints. The mechanism is simple enough to be unsettling. An ant walks. It leaves a chemical trace. Another ant follows the trace. The trace gets stronger. Soon there’s a highway where there was nothing, and no ant decided to build it.
